newsSinceJun6: No material news. Only a routine press release on presentations/1x1s at the Sidoti and Northland Capital Markets growth conferences (June 23) and the upcoming June 17 AGM. The Q1 2026 beat (revenue $33.9M, +27% y/y; return to profitability; backlog +35.5%) and the FY26 revenue guidance raise to $130-135M date to May 5 and were already reflected in yesterday's verdict. Day move -0.42% is noise.

sources.label: StockTitan - InTest to present at Sidoti, Northland conferences (June 23)
StockTitan - INTT news feed (no new items since the conference PR)
StockTitan - InTest lifts 2026 outlook after strong Q1 (8-K, May 5, 2026)

verdicts.rankRationale: No new fundamental evidence since June 6 and a flat tape (-0.42%) leave yesterday's hold intact; the only nick is rerating discipline - the stock has already doubled YTD (+106%) on a month-old guidance raise without any AI-specific packaging/burn-in design win surfacing, so the latent optionality this page rewards is slightly less mispriced than when the thesis was minted.
verdicts.bull: Tiny ($0.18B), under-followed thermal/test supplier with a fresh return to profitability, raised FY26 guidance ($130-135M) and backlog +35.5% y/y; high operating leverage if AI advanced-packaging burn-in/test demand broadens beyond the obvious semicap names.
verdicts.neutral: Q1 strength and the guidance raise are real but a month old and largely priced after the +106% YTD move; the June 23 Sidoti/Northland circuit adds visibility, not fundamentals.
verdicts.bear: Semi is only one of several end markets (auto/EV, defense, industrial); the AI test/packaging optionality is still unproven by a named win, Q1 orders ($31.8M) ran below revenue, and a doubled, thin-liquidity micro-cap gives back gains fast if semicap orders stall.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: A named AI advanced-packaging/burn-in test order or the Q2 print (early August) confirming the $32-34M guide and raised FY26 outlook; near-term visibility from the June 23 investor conferences.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Rerating exhaustion: +106% YTD on a $184M micro-cap without an AI-specific design win leaves the stock exposed to any semicap order softness, decelerating book-to-bill, or guidance walk-back.
